questions: Is the version correctly bumped to 1.3.4 in package.json?
Is the version correctly bumped to 1.3.4 in both locations in package-lock.json?
Are there any stale 1.3.3 references remaining in package metadata files?
Do typecheck and tests pass after the version bump?
Were any files modified beyond package.json and package-lock.json (workflows, source code, config)?
Does the existing pipeline (auto-tag.yml, publish.yml) correctly handle the new version?

evidence: Direct file read of package.json confirmed "version": "1.3.4" at line 3

answers.answer: Yes. package-lock.json line 3 (root level) and line 9 (packages[""] level) both read "version": "1.3.4".
evidence: Direct file read of package-lock.json confirmed "version": "1.3.4" at line 3 (root) and line 9 (packages[""])

answers.answer: No stale references. Grep for 1.3.3 in package*.json returned zero matches.
evidence: Grep pattern '1\.3\.3' with glob 'package*.json' returned 'No matches found'

answers.answer: Yes. npx tsc --noEmit exited with code 0. npm test exited with code 0 with 51 tests passing and 0 failures.
evidence: npm test: 51 pass, 0 fail, 0 skipped, duration_ms 99.4ms

answers.answer: No. Workflow files (auto-tag.yml, publish.yml) and source code (src/update/version.ts) were inspected and are unmodified. Only package.json and package-lock.json were changed.
evidence: Direct read of src/update/version.ts confirmed it reads version dynamically from package.json with no hardcoded strings

answers.answer: Yes. auto-tag.yml reads version from package.json and creates a v{version} tag only if it doesn't exist. publish.yml triggers on v* tag push and validates the tag version matches package.json. The new version 1.3.4 will cause a new tag to be created on merge to main.
evidence: auto-tag.yml lines 23-29: reads version via jq from package.json
auto-tag.yml lines 34-43: checks if tag exists on remote via git ls-remote
auto-tag.yml lines 45-52: creates and pushes tag when exists=false
publish.yml lines 32-38: validates tag version matches package.json version

statement: Code review complete. The version bump from 1.3.3 to 1.3.4 is correctly applied to package.json (line 3) and package-lock.json (lines 3, 9). No stale version references remain. Typecheck and all 51 tests pass. No workflow, source code, or config files were modified. The existing auto-tag + publish pipeline will correctly handle the new version. No issues found; no code changes needed.
